From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

System to record oil content of a ships wastewater

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Oil_discharge_monitoring_equipment "Special:EditPage/Oil discharge monitoring equipment") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Oil discharge monitoring equipment"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Oil+discharge+monitoring+equipment%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Oil+discharge+monitoring+equipment%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Oil+discharge+monitoring+equipment%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Oil+discharge+monitoring+equipment%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Oil+discharge+monitoring+equipment%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Oil+discharge+monitoring+equipment%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(June 2015)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

**Oil discharge monitoring equipment** (ODME) is based on a measurement of oil content in the ballast and slop water, to measure conformance with regulations.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The apparatus is equipped with a GPS, data recording functionality, an [oil content meter](/wiki/Oil_content_meter "Oil content meter") and a flow meter.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) By use of data interpretation, a computing unit will be able to allow the discharge to continue or it will stop it using a valve outside the deck.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

## Operating principle

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oil_discharge_monitoring_equipment&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Operating principle")]

A sample point on the discharge line allows for the analyzer to determine the oil content of the ballast now and slop water in PPM.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) The analyzer is self-maintaining by periodical cleansings with [fresh water](/wiki/Fresh_water "Fresh water"), and therefore requires a minimum of active maintenance from the crew.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) The results of the analyzer are sent to a computer, which determines whether the oil content values are to result in overboard discharge or not.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) The valves that direct the ballast water either over board or to slop tank are controlled by the integrated computer, and a GPS signal further automates the process by including special areas and completes the required input for the [Oil Record Book](/wiki/Oil_record_book "Oil record book").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

All oil tankers with a gross tonnage of larger than 150 must have efficient Oil Discharge Monitoring Equipment on board.[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The oily discharge is sent out to sea through a pump.[[5]](#cite_note-5) The oily mixture has to pass through a series of sensors to determine whether it is acceptable to be sent to the discharge pipe.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Based on regulations, the following values must be recorded by the system:[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)

* Date and time of the discharge
* Location of the ship
* Oil content of the discharge in ppm
* Total quantity discharged
* Discharge rate

All records of Oil Detection Monitoring Equipment must be stored on board ships for no less than 3 years.[[3]](#cite_note-:1-3)

Oil Discharge Monitoring systems today consist of a computing unit that is installed in the cargo control room. The computer unit control and receives data from other ODME components.[[7]](#cite_note-:2-7)

ODME systems also have an analyzing unit that contains the [Oil content meter](/wiki/Oil_content_meter "Oil content meter"), a fresh water valve for cleaning purposes, and a pressure transmitter that monitors the sample flow through the measuring cell.[[7]](#cite_note-:2-7)

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Oil_discharge_monitoring_equipment&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Marpol 73/78](/wiki/MARPOL_73/78 "MARPOL 73/78") – International marine environmental convention
* [Marpol Annex I](/wiki/Marpol_Annex_I "Marpol Annex I") – 1983 convention on ocean pollution
* [Oily water separators](/wiki/Oily_Water_Separators "Oily Water Separators") – Device to separate oil and waterPages displaying short descriptions of redirect targets
* [Oily water separator (marine)](/wiki/Oily_Water_Separator_(marine) "Oily Water Separator (marine)") – Equipment to separate oil and water mixturesPages displaying short descriptions of redirect targets
* [Oil Content Meter](/wiki/Oil_Content_Meter "Oil Content Meter") – Device for monitoring oil levels in shipboard water dischargesPages displaying short descriptions of redirect targets
* [Magic Pipe](/wiki/Magic_pipe "Magic pipe") – Trick in a ship's waste-handling equipment to circumvent pollution regulations
* [IMO](/wiki/International_Maritime_Organization "International Maritime Organization") – Specialized agency of the United Nations
* [Port Reception Facilities](/wiki/Port_Reception_Facilities "Port Reception Facilities") – Facilities in ports for receiving waste and residues from shipsPages displaying short descriptions of redirect targets
